INC-101.title: Payment gateway timeout errors
INC-101.description: Users are experiencing 504 gateway timeouts when attempting to checkout. DB pool exhaustion suspected.

INC-102.title: User avatar upload failure
INC-102.description: S3 upload fails with 403 Forbidden. Upload bucket permissions might have changed.

INC-103.title: Frontend blank page on dashboard loading
INC-103.description: Dashboard console shows 'uncaught reference error' in bundle.js.

INC-104.title: Payment checkout page returning 500 Internal Server Error
INC-104.description: Outage reported on checkout endpoint. System logs show: 'ERROR: Database Connection Pool Exhausted. Max active connections (100) exceeded.' Transactions are failing.
comments: Root Cause: Database Connection Pool Exhausted. Max active connections (100) exceeded. Resolution: Increased database connection pool size to resolve the issue.

INC-105.title: Dashboard reporting blank page on loading
INC-105.description: Users are complaining that when they log in, they see a blank page. The browser console reports a javascript reference loading failure.
comments: Incident resolved. Root cause: Syntax error: Missing closing brace in bundle.js line 452, preventing UI assets from loading for the frontend-service. Resolution: Rolled back frontend-service deployment to a stable version, or applied a hotfix for the syntax error.

INC-106.title: User profile photo upload returning 403 Forbidden
INC-106.description: Users are reporting that they cannot update their profile pictures. The browser console shows an AccessDenied network error when POSTing to the upload endpoint.
comments: Incident INC-106 (User profile photo upload returning 403 Forbidden) has been resolved. The root cause was identified as an AWS IAM permission issue: the `user-service` lacked `s3:PutObject` permissions for the `user-avatars-prod` S3 bucket. The resolution involved updating the IAM policy attached to the `user-service`'s role to explicitly grant `s3:PutObject` access to the `user-avatars-prod` S3 bucket. Subsequent tests confirmed successful profile photo uploads.
